title: execute_web_fetch and execute_web_browser duplicate output assembly logic
description:
## Severity
Minor
## Dimension
dry
## Location
src/tools/web.rs:37-58 (execute_web_fetch) and src/tools/web.rs:179-200 (execute_web_browser)
## Violation
Both tool entry points format a `FetchResult` into the agent-facing string using identical logic: title heading, URL header, content append, 50000-char truncate with "content truncated" suffix. The `50000` magic constant is duplicated.

```rust
if let Some(title) = fetch_result.title { let _ = write!(output, "# {title}\n\n"); }
let _ = write!(output, "URL: {}\n\n", fetch_result.url);
output.push_str(&fetch_result.content);
if output.len() > 50000 {
    output = format!("{}...\n\n(content truncated, {} total chars)",
        safe_truncate(&output, 50000), output.len());
}
```

The runtime-dispatch boilerplate (`Handle::try_current` -> `block_in_place` else new `Runtime`) is duplicated between `execute_web_fetch` (line 23-35) and `execute_web_search` (line 129-137).
## Mandated refactor
Extract `format_fetch_result(result: FetchResult, max_len: usize) -> String` and call from both; with `const MAX_OUTPUT_CHARS: usize = 50_000`. Extract `block_on_in_any_context<F>(fut: F) -> F::Output` for runtime dispatch.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
